evidence.display: Brief Hospital Course: . # Cardiac arrest: On admission, it was reported that he
evidence.text: Brief Hospital Course: . # Cardiac arrest: On admission, it was reported that he had a VF arrest by EMS, and converted with 1 shock in the field.

evidence.display: EP, however, felt his cardiac arrest was more likely secondary to polymorphic VT
evidence.text: EP, however, felt his cardiac arrest was more likely secondary to polymorphic VT as a result of his prolonged QT and complete heart block.

evidence.display: He had no CAD on cath so it was likely not ischemic in etiology.
evidence.text: He had no CAD on cath so it was likely not ischemic in etiology.

evidence.display: Despite the suspicion for polymorphic VT, EP felt primary VF could not be ruled
evidence.text: Despite the suspicion for polymorphic VT, EP felt primary VF could not be ruled out, so an ICD was placed on [**3-12**]. . # Complete heart block:  He had a temporary pacing wire placed at the outside hospital, and was V-paced at 60bpm.

evidence.display: His native heart rate was less than 30.
evidence.text: His native heart rate was less than 30.

evidence.display: The etiology of his complete heart block was thought to be secondary to degenera
evidence.text: The etiology of his complete heart block was thought to be secondary to degenerative conduction disease, as he seems to have had progressive conduction abnormalities per past EKGs.

evidence.display: Viral myocarditis was also considered a possibility as his family gave a history
evidence.text: Viral myocarditis was also considered a possibility as his family gave a history of flu-like symptoms a few days before his cardiac arrest.

evidence.display: His TSH was normal and his cath was clean.
evidence.text: His TSH was normal and his cath was clean.

evidence.display: A dual chamber ICD was placed on [**3-12**].
evidence.text: A dual chamber ICD was placed on [**3-12**].

evidence.display: He was maintained on a beta blocker for rate control as his rate was in the 90s
evidence.text: He was maintained on a beta blocker for rate control as his rate was in the 90s after his dual chamber ICD was placed.

evidence.display: He developed a hematoma from his ICD site, involving his left shoulder and inner
evidence.text: He developed a hematoma from his ICD site, involving his left shoulder and inner arm.

evidence.display: His heparin drip was discontinued, and his hematoma resolved into an ecchymosis
evidence.text: His heparin drip was discontinued, and his hematoma resolved into an ecchymosis that has been stable.

evidence.display: He has an appointment in device clinic for interrogation of his ICD the day afte
evidence.text: He has an appointment in device clinic for interrogation of his ICD the day after discharge.

evidence.display: He will follow up this and his other cardiac issues with his cardiologist, Dr. [
evidence.text: He will follow up this and his other cardiac issues with his cardiologist, Dr. [**Last Name (STitle) 120**]. . # AVR: He is on Coumadin as an inpatient for his mechanical aortic valve.

evidence.display: He received vitamin K 5mg po on [**3-9**] to reverse his INR in preparation for
evidence.text: He received vitamin K 5mg po on [**3-9**] to reverse his INR in preparation for his ICD placement.

evidence.display: IV heparin was restarted s/p ICD placement.
evidence.text: IV heparin was restarted s/p ICD placement.

evidence.display: His mechanical aortic valve was found to be functioning well on TTE.
evidence.text: His mechanical aortic valve was found to be functioning well on TTE.

evidence.display: He was restarted on Coumadin, and his INR was therapeutic for a few days before
evidence.text: He was restarted on Coumadin, and his INR was therapeutic for a few days before admission.

evidence.display: He was discharged on Coumadin 5mg po daily with the plan to get her INR checked
evidence.text: He was discharged on Coumadin 5mg po daily with the plan to get her INR checked 4 days after discharge.

evidence.display: He will resume management of his anticoagulation with his PCP. . # Anoxic enceph
evidence.text: He will resume management of his anticoagulation with his PCP. . # Anoxic encephalopathy:  According to the family, there was an approximate 5-minute lapse between his collapse at home and initiation of CPR.

evidence.display: He was initially minimally responsive when intubated, even with lightening of se
evidence.text: He was initially minimally responsive when intubated, even with lightening of sedation.

evidence.display: His CT head showed no intracranial hemorrhage.
evidence.text: His CT head showed no intracranial hemorrhage.

evidence.display: Fortunately, he began to recover some cortical function within 48 hours of his c
evidence.text: Fortunately, he began to recover some cortical function within 48 hours of his cardiac arrest, and began to follow commands.

evidence.display: Upon extubation and discontinuation of his sedation, he was disoriented and beca
evidence.text: Upon extubation and discontinuation of his sedation, he was disoriented and became agitated, responding well to olanzapine.

evidence.display: He was maintained on olanzapine for a couple of days, and his agitation resolved
evidence.text: He was maintained on olanzapine for a couple of days, and his agitation resolved.

evidence.display: He also seemed to have some motor difficulty with impaired hand-eye coordination
evidence.text: He also seemed to have some motor difficulty with impaired hand-eye coordination, as well as some short-term memory deficit.

evidence.display: His cognitive and motor deficits are likely due to anoxic encephalopathy.
evidence.text: His cognitive and motor deficits are likely due to anoxic encephalopathy.

evidence.display: He has experienced rapid improvement while in the hospital.
evidence.text: He has experienced rapid improvement while in the hospital.

evidence.display: He was evaluated by Occupational Therapy and Physical Therapy, and felt to be sa
evidence.text: He was evaluated by Occupational Therapy and Physical Therapy, and felt to be safe to be discharged home.

evidence.text: He will follow up with Dr. [**First Name8 (NamePattern2) **] [**Last Name (NamePattern1) **] of Behavioral Neurology in 6 weeks for reassessment. . # Respiratory status:  He was initially intubated for airway protection during cardiac arrest.

evidence.text: He had mild pulmonary edema on chest x-ray, for which he received IV Lasix for diuresis.

evidence.display: He was weaned from the ventilator over the next couple of days, and was extubate
evidence.text: He was weaned from the ventilator over the next couple of days, and was extubated on [**3-13**].

evidence.text: He had a possible infiltrate on chest x-ray and a leukocytosis, so he received a 7-day course of levofloxacin and vancomycin for empiric treatment of VAP.

evidence.text: He was weaned off oxygen and had good sats with ambulation upon discharge. . # CAD: No CAD on cath.

evidence.display: He was continued on aspirin and his outpatient dose of statin. . # CHF: He likel
evidence.text: He was continued on aspirin and his outpatient dose of statin. . # CHF: He likely has diastolic dysfunction with his aortic stenosis and hypertension.

evidence.text: His TTE showed an EF of 60%.

evidence.text: His CHF symptoms were possibly due to progressive bradycardia and complete heart block.

evidence.text: He diuresed well on IV Lasix, and was euvolemic for several days before discharge.

evidence.text: He was maintained on a BB and ACE-inhibitor. . # DM2:  His blood sugars were initially well-controlled on sliding scale insulin, but he had poor po intake.

evidence.text: He was restarted on his metformin after he resumed his diet. . # Leukocytosis:  His initial leukocytosis was felt to be secondary to a possible ventilator-associated pneumonia.

evidence.display: He was initially treated with ceftriaxone and vancomycin.
evidence.text: He was initially treated with ceftriaxone and vancomycin.

evidence.display: Ceftriaxone was discontinued for a possible drug rash.
evidence.text: Ceftriaxone was discontinued for a possible drug rash.

evidence.display: He was switched to levofloxacin and maintained on vancomycin, and completed a 7-
evidence.text: He was switched to levofloxacin and maintained on vancomycin, and completed a 7-day course of antibiotics.

evidence.display: His leukocytosis resolved initially, but then recurred, while still on antibioti
evidence.text: His leukocytosis resolved initially, but then recurred, while still on antibiotics.

evidence.display: He had no localizing symptoms, a clear chest x-ray, and negative UA and culture.
evidence.text: He had no localizing symptoms, a clear chest x-ray, and negative UA and culture.

evidence.display: As the origin for the elevated WBC count could not be identified, his antibiotic
evidence.text: As the origin for the elevated WBC count could not be identified, his antibiotics were discontinued at the end of his 7-day course for VAP.

evidence.display: He was discharged to PCP follow up, with a check of his WBC count in 4 days afte
evidence.text: He was discharged to PCP follow up, with a check of his WBC count in 4 days after discharge. . # FEN:  After extubation, he had a video swallow evaluation that showed aspiration of a regular diet.

evidence.display: He was found to tolerate ground solids and thin liquids with careful swallowing
evidence.text: He was found to tolerate ground solids and thin liquids with careful swallowing and strict aspiration precautions.

evidence.display: He was discharged home on this diet.
evidence.text: He was discharged home on this diet.

evidence.display: Speech and Swallow recommended repeat video swallow evaluation in 2 weeks to rea
evidence.text: Speech and Swallow recommended repeat video swallow evaluation in 2 weeks to reassess his aspiration risk, and hopefully clear him for regular diet. . # Code status: FULL CODE. .
